whatWasImplemented: src/app/dashboard/settings/site/rotate-key-button.tsx — client component that renders the Rotate button plus a native <dialog> modal. Modal carries the required warning copy ('widget will stop answering chat until you re-embed'). Confirm POSTs /api/sites/rotate-key, updates the displayed key via onRotated callback, shows a success toast, and calls router.refresh() so any cached server-rendered embed snippet refetches on next navigation.
Native <dialog> element for accessibility (Esc to dismiss, focus trap, backdrop click to close). No modal-library dependency.
Toast wrappers cover success + error + network failure paths; the button's 'Rotating…' spinner surfaces the in-flight state.
Replaced the M4F2-era handleRotateKey flow in site-client.tsx with <RotateKeyButton onRotated={setSiteKey} />. The inline window.confirm flow is fully retired.
whatWasLeftUndone: Live cross-tab propagation to /dashboard/embed is out of scope here. Today: rotating in Settings calls router.refresh(); visiting Embed afterwards (same or different tab) shows the new key because Embed is a server component and fetches site_key on render. Simultaneous-open-tab propagation without reload would require a Realtime subscription on sites — deferred to M8 hardening.
Playwright evidence (modal render, toast visible, new key displayed) deferred.
discoveredIssues: The legacy settings-client.tsx mint-key-on-client flow (deleted in M4F2) bypassed the server rate limit and didn't audit. Closed here — all rotate traffic now hits /api/sites/rotate-key which enforces the 5/hour user-scoped limit.

verification.testsRun: pnpm vitest run — 343 passed / 1 skipped / 49 files (no new tests for this feature — behavior is composition of the M1 API + M4F3 toast wrapper, both already covered).

manualChecks.observed: typecheck clean; visual verification pending Playwright

manualChecks.expected: toast 'Site key rotated'; key display updates; router.refresh() invalidates cached server renders
manualChecks.observed: logic verified; existing rate-limit tests prove server endpoint correctness
